specific_concerns: Mapping sat / asat to 'real customer-need' / 'phantom bug' compresses one of the most metaphysically loaded distinctions in classical Indian philosophy into an operational diagnosis. A serious practitioner might read this as collapsing Brahman / asat-tattva into a debugging checklist. The engineering layer mitigates this by explicitly preserving the literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus fields and by naming the analog as a register translation, not an ontological identity claim.
verdict_reason: The trivialization risk is real but managed. The engineering layer does not claim the Gita 'really' teaches debugging; it claims the verse's operational structure (discriminate sat from asat at the level you can actually act on) has an analog in the engineering domain. The Sanskrit terms preserved (tattva-darśin) are used as register markers, not punchlines. The dual-tradition disagreement (Advaita vs Vishishtadvaita) is preserved in the source pack rather than flattened. PASS with a non-zero severity flag noting the inherent risk in mapping ontological vocabulary to operational vocabulary.
